violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Owner informed inspector that one manager has registered for on-line training. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen; a rag was in the hand-washing sink in the dish room. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Items were removed during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
The hand-washing sink in the dish wash area was lacking paper towels. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (beverages) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Staff were observed rinsing dishes and placing with clean dishes without washing or sanitizing. PIC informed, 3-bay sink set-up and dishes were washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Cheese was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit above the prep unit fill line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cheese was placed below the fill line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
Time for Public Health Control logs were incomplete, having no information since 7/25/25. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
A deceased rodent was observed in a trap in the kitchen. PIC informed to remove. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings throughout the facility and a deceased rodent. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC stated more dunnage racks were being ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Rinsed dishes were not allowed to air dry prior to stacking. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the large stand mixer and small mixer bowl have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Items taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
There was a visible gap in the wall where the stainless-steel covering meets drywall junctures in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

The following have an accumulation of soil residue, food and grease debris, and rodent droppings: -floor/wall junctures throughout the facility -under equipment -walls under ventilation hoods -above walk-in cooler -behind water tank -utility shelf over stairway. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
An employee's phone was observed stored on prep unit. PIC moved phone during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Inspector and PIC diluted to proper concentration during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the large stand mixer and under the prep unit lid have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

The floors and walls in the throughout the facility has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris at the floor/wall junctures. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

Hot dogs were observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit, with prep unit ambient temperature at 54 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC adjusted temperature during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024
Records are not being maintained for tapioca pearls (boba) as required by RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-ins and the dry storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Inspector observed cases of single service items on the floor in the dry storage area. Single service items must be stored 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Condenser located in the walk-in cooler was not in good repair. Inspector observed condenser wrapped in plastic, with water dripping from the plastic wrap. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets on the front service prep unit was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -prep unit doors and gaskets -walk-in freezer gaskets -low boy doors and gaskets -exterior of the ice machine -stand mixer. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Establishment had an accumulation of used fryer oil waiting to be disposed of. Per PIC, the receptable used for the building had not been emptied for more than two weeks. PIC and inspector observed the area and found tank had been emptied. PIC began removal of used oil during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and/or rodent droppings:-space over walk-in cooler-hand sinks-floors and walls throughout facility.The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023

Gloves, bucket and scrub pad were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023

Tapioca pearls were observed held at 108 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Hot holding unit was adjusted to proper temperature at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023
Beverages and food were observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023

The middle prep room does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2023
Plumbing for 3 bay sink faucet was observed leaking. System to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2023
